person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Gilbert Crispin is not the same as Gilbert Comte de Brionne and d'Eu.

There is a great deal of disagreement and there has been much confusion about one or several Gilberts (Giselbertus) who were living in Normandy in the early 11th century, one or more of whom may have been called "Crispin". It appears that some historical sources have merged all of these into one while others keep them separate. The same children (and wives, in many cases) have been attributed to any and all, which males it very difficult to establish any credible family lineages.

Brionne (Brienne), COUNT OF EU AND BRIONNE, 2nd Count of Brionne, Count of Eu, Comte, d'Eu, de Brionne, Comte De Brionne, Count Eu Brion, Comte de Brionne, Count, Lord Of Tillie and Gunnore D' Ainon, Lord of Tillie
LESSGilbert, Count of Brionne From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia he inherited Brionne, becoming one of the most powerful landowners in Normandy. He married Gunnora d'Aunou (Gunmore d'Ainon) in 1012. He had children by his wife and a mistress. References The Royal Ancestry Bible Royal ancestors of 300 American Families By Michel L. Call ISBN 1-933194-22-7 (chart 1696) Another account is below claims Gilbert's mother was a mistress who was also mistress to Robert the Magnificent. 3 The Domesday Book Online www.domesdaybook.co.uk Richard fitz Gilbert's mother, Herleve, daughter of Fulbert the tanner, came from Falaise and rose to a prominent status in French nobility despite her ordinary upbringing. At the age of 16 she met Gilbert, Count of Brionne, who fathered Richard fitz Gilbert with her. A year or so later, she gave birth to William the Conqueror, who was fathered by Robert "The Devil" Duke of Normandy. Shortly before Duke Robert's death in 1035, she was persuaded by him to marry Herluin, Viscount of Conteville. By him she bore two sons, Odo, Bishop of Bayeux, and Robert, Count of Mortain. Both men, along with Richard fitz Gilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and played major roles in the conquest of England 3.
LESS


Gilbert (or Giselbert) de Brionne, 2nd Count of Eu, 2nd Count of Brionne (c.?1000-1040), was an influential Norman Nobleman in the Duchy of Normandy in Northern France. He was one of the early guardians of Duke William II in his minority. Had Lord Brionne not been murdered, the senior house of de Clare would probably have been titled de Brionne. Lord Brionne was the first to be known by the cognomen Crispin because of his hair style which stood up like the branches of a pine tree.

Life 

Gilbert de Brionne was son of Geoffrey de Brionne, Count of Eu (born 962) who was an illegitimate child of Richard I of Normandy. He inherited Brionne, becoming one of the most powerful landowners in Normandy. Gilbert was a generous benefactor to Bec Abbey founded by his former knight Herluin in 1031. When Robert I died in 1035, his illegitimate son William inherited his father's title and several powerful nobles, including Gilbert of Brionne, Osbern the Seneschal and Alan of Brittany, became William's guardians.

Death 

A number of Norman barons, including Ralph de Gacé, refused to accept William as their leader. In 1040 an attempt was made to kill William but the plot failed. Gilbert however was murdered while he was peaceably riding near Eschafour. It is believed two of his killers were Ralph of Wacy and Robert de Vitot. This appears to have been an act of vengeance for the wrongs inflicted upon the orphan children of Giroie by Gilbert, and it is not clear what Ralph de Gacé had to do in the business. Fearing they might meet their father's fate, Gilbert's sons Richard and Baldwin were conveyed by their friends to the court of Baldwin V, Count of Flanders. His children would accompany Duke William on his conquest of England and his descendants would become one of the most powerful noble families in the British isles. They would rule over vast lands in modern-day Ireland, Scotland, and England and become powerful Marcher Lords who acted independently of the crown.

Children

1.) Sir Richard Fitz Gilbert (Richard de Clare) (bef. 1035-1090), m. Rohese Giffard (1034-aft. 1113), daughter of Walter Giffard, Lord of Longueville
2.) Baldwin FitzGilbert (d. 1090)
3.) William (-after 29 Aug 1060)
4.) Adela (-Aug 1092), m. Neel II, viscount of Cotentin (fr)

Through his eldest son, Gilbert was ancestor of the English house of de Clare, of the Barons FitzWalter, and the Earls of Gloucester (see Earl of Gloucester) and Hertford (see Earl of Hertford). After Gilbert's death, his uncle William I became Count of Eu whereas Brionne reverted to the duke.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne於大約1000年出生在法國諾門迪艾布利奧安時，他的父親Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne48歲，母親Hawise Guisnes de Brionne43歲。他和Gunnora d' Aunou至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1040年11月24日在法國巴-諾門迪奧內艾尚奧福爾享年41歲，埋葬在法國巴-諾門迪奧內艾尚奧福爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne was born about 1000, in Brionne, Eure, Normandy, France, his father, Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne, was 48 and his mother, Hawise Guisnes de Brionne, was 43. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Gunnora d' Aunou. He died on 24 November 1040, in Échauffour, Orne, Lower Normandy, France, at the age of 41, and was buried in Échauffour, Orne, Lower Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne est né environ 1000, à Brionne, Eure, Normandie, France, son père, Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Hawise Guisnes de Brionne, avait 43 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Gunnora d' Aunou. Il est décédé le 24 novembre 1040, à Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne um 1000 in Brionne, Eure, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne 48 und seine Mutter Hawise Guisnes de Brionne 43. Er hatte mit Gunnora d' Aunou mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 24. November 1040 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne è nato il circa 1000 nel Brionne, Eure, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Hawise Guisnes de Brionne, aveva 43 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Gunnora d' Aunou. Morì il 24 novembre 1040, nel Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolto a Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionneが約1000年にフランスノーマンディエウアブリオンで生まれた時，父Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionneは48歳，母Hawise Guisnes de Brionneは43歳であった。彼はGunnora d' Aunouとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1040年11月24日にフランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンeチャウフォウルで41歳で死去し，フランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンeチャウフォウルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne(이)가 대략 1000년 프랑스 노먼디 우르 브리오니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Hawise Guisnes de Brionne(은)는 43세였다. 그는 Gunnora d' Aunou(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1040년 11월 24일 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 e차포에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 e차포에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne nasceu aproximadamente 1000, em Brionne, Eure, Normandie, França, seu pai, Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Hawise Guisnes de Brionne, tinha 43 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Gunnora d' Aunou. Ele faleceu em 24 de novembro de 1040, em Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, França, com 41 anos, e foi sepultado em Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne родился около 1000 г. в местности брионн, эур, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Hawise Guisnes de Brionne, 43. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Gunnora d' Aunou. Он умер 24 ноября 1040 г. в местности eчауффоур, орн, бассе-норманди, Франция в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности eчауффоур, орн, бассе-норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gilbert de Brionne comte d'Eu et de Brionne nació aproximadamente 1000, en Brionne, Eure, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Godfrey Comte d'Eu et Brionne, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Hawise Guisnes de Brionne, tenía 43 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Gunnora d' Aunou. Murió el 24 de noviembre de 1040, en Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultado en Échauffour, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia.
